HomiCSx

  • homicsx.core
  • View page source

homicsx.core

class homicsx.core.Inclusion(center, phase_id, shape, radii, interphase_thickness_ratio=0.0, interphase_phase_id=None, periodic_source_id=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Represents a geometric inclusion (particle) within the RVE.

This class defines the geometry, phase properties, and interphase characteristics of a single inclusion. It supports both 2D and 3D shapes, including circular, spherical, and ellipsoidal geometries, with built-in support for periodic boundary conditions and interphase layers.

Variables:

  • center (np.ndarray) – The coordinates of the inclusion center (2D or 3D).

  • phase_id (int) – Material phase identifier for the inclusion core.

  • shape (SupportedShape) – Geometry type. Must be one of: “circle”, “sphere”, “ellipse”, “ellipsoid”.

  • radii (np.ndarray) – Geometric dimensions. Represents the radius for circles/spheres, or semi-axes for ellipses/ellipsoids.

  • interphase_thickness_ratio (float, optional) – Ratio of the interphase thickness relative to the total radius/semi-axes. Must be in the range [0.0, 1.0). Default is 0.0 (no interphase).

  • interphase_phase_id (int | None, optional) – Material phase identifier for the interphase layer. If not provided and thickness ratio > 0, defaults to phase_id + 100.

  • periodic_source_id (int | None, optional) – The ID of the original inclusion if this instance is a periodic image across the RVE boundaries. Default is None.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any], optional) – Additional user-defined data associated with the inclusion.

Raises:

ValueError – If dimensions, phase IDs, or radii are inconsistent with the specified shape.

property dim

The spatial dimension (2 or 3) of the inclusion.

property radius

If shape is circle of sphere, returns radius.

property has_interphase

True if the inclusion has an active interphase layer.

property inner_radii

Inner radii of the inclusion. Same as outer radii if there is no interphase.

property outer_radii

Outer radii of the inclusion.

property volume_measure

Total volume of the RVE.

property core_volume

Volume of the core of the inclusion, excluding the interphase layer from the total volume.

property interphase_volume

Volume of the interphase, excluding the core from the total volume.

property total_volume

Total volume (or area in 2D) of the inclusion including its interphase.

is_periodic_image()[source]

Returns true if inclusion is a periodic image.

class homicsx.core.GeometryInput(dim, dispersion, shape, volume_fraction, clearance, domain_size=1, volume_fraction_tolerance=None, num_particles=None, axis_ratios=None, min_radius=None, max_radius=None, min_scale=None, max_scale=None, max_restarts=100, attempts_per_restart=100000, seed=None, interphase_thickness_ratio=0.0, verbosity=0)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration container for RVE geometry generation.

This class holds all physical and algorithmic parameters required to generate a Representative Volume Element (RVE).

Variables:

  • dim (int) – Spatial dimension of the RVE. Must be 2 or 3.

  • dispersion (str) – Type of particle size distribution. Options: “mono” (uniform size) or “poly” (random size within a range).

  • shape (str) – Geometry of the inclusions. 3D options: “sphere”, “ellipsoid”. 2D options: “circle”, “ellipse”.

  • volume_fraction (float) – Target total volume fraction of the inclusions (range: [0, 1]).

  • clearance (float) – Minimum allowable distance between the surfaces of any two inclusions.

  • domain_size (float | tuple | list | np.ndarray, default 1.0) – Dimensions of the RVE bounding box. If a single float is provided, a cubic/square domain is assumed.

  • volume_fraction_tolerance (float, optional) – Allowable deviation from the target volume fraction. Required for “poly” dispersion, must be None for “mono”.

  • num_particles (int, optional) – Fixed number of particles to generate. Required for “mono”, must be None for “poly”.

  • axis_ratios (tuple[int], optional) – Ratios of semi-axes for ellipsoidal/elliptical shapes.

  • max_radius (min_radius,) – The range for primary radius/semi-axis generation. Required for “poly”, must be None for “mono”.

  • max_scale (min_scale,) – Scaling factors for particle sizes in polydisperse systems.

  • max_restarts (int, default 100) – Maximum number of global attempts to satisfy the volume fraction.

  • attempts_per_restart (int, default 100000) – Number of random placement trials before restarting or failing.

  • seed (int, optional) – Seed for the random number generator to ensure reproducibility.

  • interphase_thickness_ratio (float, default 0.0) – Relative thickness of the interphase layer surrounding each inclusion. If set to 0.0, no interphase layer will be implemented.

  • verbosity (int, default 0) – Level of logging output during the generation process (0 for silent).

Note

The class enforces strict mutual exclusivity between “mono” and “poly” modes. In “mono” mode, the number of particles is fixed, while in “poly” mode, the generator attempts to fill the volume until the volume_fraction (within tolerance) is reached using a size distribution.

Raises:

ValueError – If invalid dimensions, shapes, or conflicting dispersion parameters are provided.

class homicsx.core.RVEGeometry(dim, domain_size, inclusions=<factory>, phase_ids=(0, 1), target_volume_fraction=None, realized_volume_fraction=None, seed=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Represents the micro-structure of a representative volume element (RVE) collected from a macroscopic composite body. An object of this class contains the domain definition and all of the inclusion data (including the periodic images) inside the domain required to build the mesh.

Variables:

  • dim (int) – States the spacial dimention of the RVE (2 for 2D and 3 for 3D)

  • domain_size (np.ndarray) – States the size of the domain in its dimensions: - Of size 2 and including Lx and Ly for 2D domains. - Of size 3 and including Lx, Ly, and Lz for 3D domains.

  • inclusions (list[Inclusion]) – Contains all of the inclusion data, including the centers, radii, phase IDs, shapes, and source IDs if periodic.

  • phase_ids (tuple[int, ]) – Tuple of material phase identifiers present in the RVE. The first entry ID is treated as the matrix phase. Defults to (0, 1) as a 2 phase composite.

  • target_volume_fraction (float) – The input target volume fraction intended for the generated RVE.

  • realized_volume_fraction (float) – The output realized volume fraction of the RVE. Intended to equal to ‘target_volume_fraction’.

  • seed (int) – The random seed used in the RVE generation process.

  • metadata (dict) – Optional dictionary for additional properties.

Note

  • An object of this class explicitly contains the periodic images inside ‘inclusions’, if available.

  • Domain is assumed to start at origin.

  • No geometric validation (eg. overlap checks) is enforced here.

See also

Inclusion

property domain_measure

The total area (2D) or volume (3D) of the RVE domain.

property core_volume_fraction

The volume fraction of only the inclusion cores (excluding interphases).

property interphase_volume_fraction

The volume fraction occupied by the interphase layers.

property total_inclusion_volume_fraction

The sum of core and interphase volume fractions.

add_inclusion(inclusion)[source]

Add an inclusion of type Inclusion.

class homicsx.core.PhysicalTags(matrix=1, phase_tag_offset=10, left=3, right=4, bottom=5, top=6, near=7, far=8)[source]

Bases: object

Defines physical-tag convention for the mesh module.

It centralizes the tag ID for both cell entities (matrix and inclusion phases) and the boundary facets. It ensures consistent physical tagging across GMSH and DOLFINx.

Variables:

  • matrix (int) – The physical tag used for matrix region. By default set to 1.

  • phase_tag_offset (int) – The offset used to distinguish and separate the tags used for matrix/boundary entities and inclusion phases. phase_tag = phase_id + phase_tag_offset

  • left (int) – The physical tag assigned to the left boundary (x = 0). Fixed to 3.

  • right (int) – The physical tag assigned to the right boundary (x = Lx). Fixed to 4.

  • bottom (int) – The physical tag assigned to the bottom boundary (y = 0). Fixed to 5.

  • top (int) – The physical tag assigned to the top boundary (y = Ly). Fixed to 6.

  • near (int) – The physical tag assigned to the near boundary (z = 0, only in 3D). Fixed to 7.

  • far (int) – The physical tag assigned to the far boundary (z = Lz, only in 3D). Fixed to 8.

Note

  • Boundary physical tags are fixed and dimensional-dependent.

cell_tag_for_phase(phase_id, matrix_phase_id=0)[source]

Calculates the unique integer tag for a given material phase.

Parameters:

  • phase_id (int) – The ID of the phase to tag.

  • matrix_phase_id (int, default 0) – The ID that represents the matrix in the geometry model.

Returns:

The physical tag (either matrix or offset + phase_id).

Return type:

int

cell_name_for_phase(phase_id, matrix_phase_id=0, is_interphase=False)[source]

Generates a human-readable string name for a material phase.

Parameters:

  • phase_id (int) – The ID of the phase.

  • matrix_phase_id (int, default 0) – The ID designated as the matrix.

  • is_interphase (bool, default False) – Whether the phase represents an interphase layer.

Returns:

A string name like “Matrix”, “Core_Phase_1”, or “Interphase_Phase_101”.

Return type:

str

boundary_name_to_tag(dim)[source]

Returns a mapping of boundary names to their corresponding integer tags.

Parameters:

dim (int) – Dimension of the problem (2 or 3).

Returns:

A dictionary where keys are boundary names (e.g., “left”, “top”) and values are their assigned physical tags.

Return type:

dict[str, int]

Raises:

ValueError – If dim is not 2 or 3.

class homicsx.core.MeshSettings(min_size, max_size, model_rank=0, model_name='rve', physical_tags=None, boundary_tol=1e-08, refine_interfaces=True, interface_dist_min=None, interface_dist_max=None, verbosity=0, optimize=True, smoothing_steps=10, view=False, finalize=True)[source]

Bases: object

Container for GMSH mesh generator input parameters and settings.

Variables:

  • min_size (float) – Minimum mesh element size, typically used near material interfaces.

  • max_size (float) – Maximum mesh element size, typically used in bulk regions.

  • comm (mpi4py.MPI.Comm, optional) – MPI communicator used for mesh conversion and distribution. Defaults to MPI.COMM_WORLD.

  • model_rank (int, optional) – Rank responsible for constructing the Gmsh model and generating the mesh. Defaults to 0.

  • model_name (str, optional) – Name assigned to the Gmsh model. Defaults to "rve".

  • physical_tags (PhysicalTags or None, optional) – Tagging convention for physical groups. If None, defaults are used.

  • boundary_tol (float, optional) – Tolerance used for outer boundary classification. Defaults to 1e-8.

  • refine_interfaces (bool, optional) – If True, enable simple refinement near material interfaces. Defaults to True.

  • interface_dist_min (float or None, optional) – Distance below which the minimum mesh size is enforced in the interface refinement field.

  • interface_dist_max (float or None, optional) – Distance beyond which the maximum mesh size is enforced in the interface refinement field.

  • verbosity (int, optional) – Gmsh verbosity level. Defaults to 0.

  • optimize (bool, optional) – If True, enable Gmsh mesh optimization. Defaults to True.

  • smoothing_steps (int, optional) – Number of mesh smoothing iterations requested from Gmsh. Defaults to 10.

  • view (bool, optional) – If True, open the Gmsh GUI viewer after mesh generation on the model-owning rank. Defaults to False.

  • finalize (bool, optional) – If True, finalize Gmsh before returning. Defaults to True.

class homicsx.core.LinearElasticIsotropic(young_modulus, poisson_ratio, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Isotropic small-strain linear-elastic material.

This is the standard linear-elastic material model currently used for heterogeneous elasticity problems in HomiCSx. It is defined by Young’s modulus and Poisson’s ratio and is intended for phases that follow an infinitesimal-strain constitutive law.

Variables:

  • young_modulus (float) – Young’s modulus E of the material.

  • poisson_ratio (float) – Poisson’s ratio nu of the material.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any]) – Optional free-form dictionary for additional information.

class homicsx.core.NonlinearMaterialModel[source]

Bases: object

Abstract base class for nonlinear hyperelastic material models.

This class defines the interface for material models that provide a strain energy density function (Psi), which is used to derive the constitutive response in finite strain simulations.

psi_form(F=None, u=None, **kwargs)[source]

Computes the strain energy density expression.

Parameters:

  • F (ufl.Form, optional) – The deformation gradient tensor.

  • u (dolfinx.fem.Function, optional) – The displacement field.

Raises:

NotImplementedError – If the subclass does not implement this method.

class homicsx.core.NeoHookeanIsotropic(young_modulus, poisson_ratio, name='NeoHookeanIsotropic', metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: NonlinearMaterialModel

Neo-Hookean finite-strain nonlinear-elastic material.

It is defined by Young’s modulus and Poisson’s ratio and is intended for phases that follow a finite-strain constitutive law.

Variables:

  • young_modulus (float) – Young’s modulus E of the material.

  • poisson_ratio (float) – Poisson’s ratio nu of the material.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any]) – Optional free-form dictionary for additional information.

property mu

Shear modulus

property lmbda

First Lame parameter

psi_form(F)[source]

Compressible Neo-Hookean strain energy density.

Parameters:

F (UFL tensor) – Deformation gradient.

Returns:

Strain energy density.

Return type:

UFL expression

class homicsx.core.MaterialAssignment(materials_by_phase, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Map phase ids to material models.

HomiCSx geometry is phase-based, so the most natural place to assign material models is at the phase-id level. This object keeps that mapping explicit and lightweight.

Variables:

  • materials_by_phase (dict[int, object]) – Dictionary mapping each phase id to a concrete material object such as LinearElasticIsotropic or NeoHookeanIsotropic.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any]) – Optional free-form dictionary for bookkeeping or future extensions.

Note

  • The matrix is usually phase_id = 0, but that convention is not enforced here.

  • Different phases may use different constitutive models.

  • The FEM assembly layer later decides whether the global problem is linear or nonlinear based on the assigned materials.

class homicsx.core.ProblemSettings(dim, kinematics='small_strain', two_dimensional_formulation=None, element_family='Lagrange', element_degree=1, quadrature_degree=None, petsc_options=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Basic settings for continuum-mechanics FEM problems in HomiCSx.

Variables:

  • dim (int) – Geometric dimension of the mesh. Supported values are 2 and 3.

  • kinematics (str) –

    Name of the kinematic setting. Typical values are:

    • ”small_strain”

    • ”finite_strain”

    This is mainly descriptive metadata. The actual global problem type is inferred from the assigned material models during assembly.

  • two_dimensional_formulation (str | None) – Optional label for 2D problems. For now, “plane_strain” is the main intended use. Use None for 3D.

  • element_family (str) – Finite-element family used for the displacement field.

  • element_degree (int) – Polynomial degree of the displacement field.

  • quadrature_degree (int | None) – Optional quadrature degree, stored here for future use.

  • petsc_options (dict[str, Any] | None) – Optional dictionary of PETSc or solver options.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any]) – Optional free-form dictionary for bookkeeping or later extensions.

class homicsx.core.LinearProblemDefinition(function_space, trial_function, test_function, bilinear_form, linear_form, bcs, mpc, solution, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Container for a linear variational problem.

Variables:

  • function_space (dolfinx.fem.FunctionSpace) – Displacement function space.

  • trial_function (dolfinx.fem.Function) – Trial function of the variational problem.

  • test_function (dolfinx.fem.Function) – Test function of the variational problem.

  • bilinear_form (ufl.Form) – Bilinear form a(u, v).

  • linear_form (ufl.Form) – Linear form L(v).

  • bcs (list[dolfinx.fem.DirichletBC]) – List of Dirichlet boundary conditions.

  • mpc (dolfinx_mpc.MultiPointConstraint | None) – Optional multi-point constraint object.

  • solution (dolfinx.fem.Function) – Displacement function used to store the solution.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any]) – Optional free-form dictionary for bookkeeping.

Note

Keeping these values together makes the downstream solve step easier to read and avoids passing many related FEM objects around separately.

class homicsx.core.SolveResult(solution, converged, num_iterations, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Result returned by linear solver wrappers.

Variables:

  • solution (Any) – Final solved displacement field.

  • converged (bool) – Whether the solver reported convergence.

  • num_iterations (int | None) – Number of solver iterations if available. For direct linear solvers, this may be None.

  • metadata (dict[str, Any]) – Optional free-form dictionary containing solver information.

Note

This result object stays intentionally compact. It is mainly meant to carry the solved field together with a few important status values.

class homicsx.core.HomogenizationResult(C_hom, load_cases, average_stresses, fluctuation_fields, metadata)[source]

Bases: object

Result of a linear homogenization run.

Variables:

  • C_hom (np.ndarray) – Homogenized stiffness matrix in Voigt notation.

  • load_cases (list[np.ndarray]) – List of macroscopic strain tensors used in the solve loop.

  • average_stresses (list[np.ndarray]) – List of volume-averaged stress vectors, one per load case.

  • fluctuation_fields (list[any]) – Solved periodic fluctuation fields, one per load case.

  • metadata (dict[str, any]) – Optional bookkeeping dictionary.

class homicsx.core.EnsembleStatSummary(engineering_moduli_mean, engineering_moduli_variance, engineering_moduli_stdev, engineering_moduli_max, engineering_moduli_min, C_hom_component_avg, C_hom_component_variance, C_hom_component_stdev, C_hom_component_max, C_hom_component_min)[source]

Bases: object

Conteiner for summary of results of an ensemble study.

Variables:

  • engineering_moduli_mean (dict[str, float]) – keys: “E” for Yong modulus, “nu” for Poison ratio, “kappa” for bulk modulus, and “mu” for shear modulus.

  • engineering_moduli_variance (dict[str, float]) – keys: “E” for Yong modulus, “nu” for Poison ratio, “kappa” for bulk modulus, and “mu” for shear modulus.

  • engineering_moduli_stdev (dict[str, float]) – keys: “E” for Yong modulus, “nu” for Poison ratio, “kappa” for bulk modulus, and “mu” for shear modulus.

  • engineering_moduli_max (dict[str, float]) – keys: “E” for Yong modulus, “nu” for Poison ratio, “kappa” for bulk modulus, and “mu” for shear modulus.

  • engineering_moduli_min (dict[str, float]) – keys: “E” for Yong modulus, “nu” for Poison ratio, “kappa” for bulk modulus, and “mu” for shear modulus.

  • C_hom_component_avg (np.ndarray) – Component-wise mean of homogenizaed stiffness tensor.

  • C_hom_component_variance (np.ndarray) – Component-wise variance of homogenizaed stiffness tensor. Defined if size of ensemble study is larger than 2.

  • C_hom_component_stdev (np.ndarray) – Component-wise standard-deviation of homogenizaed stiffness tensor. Defined if size of ensemble study is larger than 2.

  • C_hom_component_max (np.ndarray) – Component-wise max of homogenizaed stiffness tensor.

  • C_hom_component_min (np.ndarray) – Component-wise min of homogenizaed stiffness tensor.

class homicsx.core.EnsembleStudyResult(result_list, metadata=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Container for ensemble of linear study results, with built in summarizing and visualization utilities.

Variables:

  • result_list (list[`HomigenizationResult]`) – Contains the results of the ensemble study in its entirety.

  • result_suammry (EnsembleStatSummary) – Contains the summary of the nesemble study.

See also

EnsembleStatSummary

result_moduli()[source]

Returns result moduli as a dict with keys “E”, “nu”, “kappa”, “mu”, and list of values as values.

print_summary()[source]

Prints the auto-generated summary of the ensemble study results.

visualize_moduli_histogram(num_bins)[source]

Visualizes moduli distribution using histogram charts.
